channel.title: kernel.org — recent CVEs
channel.description: Most recent CVEs assigned by kernel.org (linux).

item.title: CVE-2026-53130 — fs/omfs: reject s_sys_blocksize smaller than OMFS_DIR_START
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: fs/omfs: reject s_sys_blocksize smaller than OMFS_DIR_START omfs_fill_super() rejects oversized s_sys_blocksize values (> PAGE_SIZE), but it does not reject values smaller than OMFS_…

item.title: CVE-2026-53129 — fs/mbcache: cancel shrink work before destroying the cache
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: fs/mbcache: cancel shrink work before destroying the cache mb_cache_destroy() calls shrinker_free() and then frees all cache entries and the cache itself, but it does not cancel the…

item.title: CVE-2026-53128 — drbd: Balance RCU calls in drbd_adm_dump_devices()
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: drbd: Balance RCU calls in drbd_adm_dump_devices() Make drbd_adm_dump_devices() call rcu_read_lock() before rcu_read_unlock() is called. This has been detected by the Clang thread-sa…

item.title: CVE-2026-53127 — block: fix zones_cond memory leak on zone revalidation error paths
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: block: fix zones_cond memory leak on zone revalidation error paths When blk_revalidate_disk_zones() fails after disk_revalidate_zone_resources() has allocated args.zones_cond, the me…

item.title: CVE-2026-53126 — blk-cgroup: fix disk reference leak in blkcg_maybe_throttle_current()
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: blk-cgroup: fix disk reference leak in blkcg_maybe_throttle_current() Add the missing put_disk() on the error path in blkcg_maybe_throttle_current(). When blkcg lookup, blkg lookup,…

item.title: CVE-2026-53125 — md: fix array_state=clear sysfs deadlock
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: md: fix array_state=clear sysfs deadlock When "clear" is written to array_state, md_attr_store() breaks sysfs active protection so the array can delete itself from its own sysfs stor…

item.title: CVE-2026-53124 — ublk: reset per-IO canceled flag on each fetch
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: ublk: reset per-IO canceled flag on each fetch If a ublk server starts recovering devices but dies before issuing fetch commands for all IOs, cancellation of the fetch commands that…

item.title: CVE-2026-53123 — md: wake raid456 reshape waiters before suspend
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: md: wake raid456 reshape waiters before suspend During raid456 reshape, direct IO across the reshape position can sleep in raid5_make_request() waiting for reshape progress while sti…

item.title: CVE-2026-53122 — btrfs: fix deadlock between reflink and transaction commit when using flushoncommit
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: btrfs: fix deadlock between reflink and transaction commit when using flushoncommit When using the flushoncommit mount option, we can have a deadlock between a transaction commit and…

item.title: CVE-2026-53121 — amd-pstate: Fix memory leak in amd_pstate_epp_cpu_init()
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: amd-pstate: Fix memory leak in amd_pstate_epp_cpu_init() On failure to set the epp, the function amd_pstate_epp_cpu_init() returns with an error code without freeing the cpudata obje…

item.title: CVE-2026-53120 — PCI: use generic driver_override infrastructure
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: PCI: use generic driver_override infrastructure When a driver is probed through __driver_attach(), the bus' match() callback is called without the device lock held, thus accessing th…

item.title: CVE-2026-53119 — platform/wmi: use generic driver_override infrastructure
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: platform/wmi: use generic driver_override infrastructure When a driver is probed through __driver_attach(), the bus' match() callback is called without the device lock held, thus acc…

item.title: CVE-2026-53118 — vdpa: use generic driver_override infrastructure
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: vdpa: use generic driver_override infrastructure When a driver is probed through __driver_attach(), the bus' match() callback is called without the device lock held, thus accessing t…

item.title: CVE-2026-53117 — s390/cio: use generic driver_override infrastructure
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: s390/cio: use generic driver_override infrastructure When a driver is probed through __driver_attach(), the bus' match() callback is called without the device lock held, thus accessi…

item.title: CVE-2026-53116 — s390/ap: use generic driver_override infrastructure
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: s390/ap: use generic driver_override infrastructure When the AP masks are updated via apmask_store() or aqmask_store(), ap_bus_revise_bindings() is called after ap_attr_mutex has bee…

item.title: CVE-2026-53115 — bus: fsl-mc: use generic driver_override infrastructure
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: bus: fsl-mc: use generic driver_override infrastructure When a driver is probed through __driver_attach(), the bus' match() callback is called without the device lock held, thus acce…

item.title: CVE-2026-53114 — perf/amd/ibs: Avoid calling perf_allow_kernel() from the IBS NMI handler
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: perf/amd/ibs: Avoid calling perf_allow_kernel() from the IBS NMI handler Calling perf_allow_kernel() from the NMI context is unsafe and could be fatal. Capture the permission at even…

item.title: CVE-2026-53113 — wifi: ath11k: fix memory leaks in beacon template setup
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: wifi: ath11k: fix memory leaks in beacon template setup The functions ath11k_mac_setup_bcn_tmpl_ema() and ath11k_mac_setup_bcn_tmpl_mbssid() allocate memory for beacon templates but…

item.title: CVE-2026-53112 — wifi: rtlwifi: pci: fix possible use-after-free caused by unfinished irq_prepare_bcn_tasklet
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: wifi: rtlwifi: pci: fix possible use-after-free caused by unfinished irq_prepare_bcn_tasklet The irq_prepare_bcn_tasklet is initialized in rtl_pci_init() and scheduled when RTL_IMR_B…

item.title: CVE-2026-53111 — bpf: test_run: Fix the null pointer dereference issue in bpf_lwt_xmit_push_encap
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: bpf: test_run: Fix the null pointer dereference issue in bpf_lwt_xmit_push_encap The bpf_lwt_xmit_push_encap helper needs to access skb_dst(skb)->dev to calculate the needed headroom…

item.title: CVE-2026-53110 — s390/bpf: Zero-extend bpf prog return values and kfunc arguments
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: s390/bpf: Zero-extend bpf prog return values and kfunc arguments s390x ABI requires callers to zero-extend unsigned arguments and sign-extend signed arguments, and callees to zero-ex…

item.title: CVE-2026-53109 — powerpc/pgtable-frag: Fix bad page state in pte_frag_destroy
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: powerpc/pgtable-frag: Fix bad page state in pte_frag_destroy powerpc uses pt_frag_refcount as a reference counter for tracking it's pte and pmd page table fragments. For PTE table, i…

item.title: CVE-2026-53108 — powerpc/64s: Fix unmap race with PMD migration entries
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: powerpc/64s: Fix unmap race with PMD migration entries The following race is possible with migration swap entries or device-private THP entries. e.g. when move_pages is called on a P…

item.title: CVE-2026-53107 — wifi: libertas: don't kill URBs in interrupt context
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: wifi: libertas: don't kill URBs in interrupt context Serialization for the TX path was enforced by calling usb_kill_urb()/usb_kill_anchored_urbs(), to prevent transmission before a p…

item.title: CVE-2026-53106 — bpf: Do not allow deleting local storage in NMI
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: bpf: Do not allow deleting local storage in NMI Currently, local storage may deadlock when deferring freeing selem or local storage through kfree_rcu(), call_rcu() or call_rcu_tasks_…

item.title: CVE-2026-53105 — wifi: mt76: mt7925: prevent NULL vif dereference in mt7925_mac_write_txwi
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: wifi: mt76: mt7925: prevent NULL vif dereference in mt7925_mac_write_txwi Check for a NULL `vif` before accessing `ieee80211_vif_is_mld(vif)` to avoid a potential kernel panic in sce…

item.title: CVE-2026-53104 — wifi: mt76: Fix memory leak destroying device
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: wifi: mt76: Fix memory leak destroying device All MT76 rx queues have an associated page_pool even if the queue is not associated to a NAPI (e.g. WED RRO queues with WED enabled). De…

item.title: CVE-2026-53103 — wifi: mt76: mt7925: fix potential deadlock in mt7925_roc_abort_sync
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: wifi: mt76: mt7925: fix potential deadlock in mt7925_roc_abort_sync roc_abort_sync() can deadlock with roc_work(). roc_work() holds dev->mt76.mutex, while cancel_work_sync() waits fo…

item.title: CVE-2026-53102 — wifi: mt76: Fix memory leak after mt76_connac_mcu_alloc_sta_req()
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: wifi: mt76: Fix memory leak after mt76_connac_mcu_alloc_sta_req() mt76_connac_mcu_alloc_sta_req() allocates an skb which is expected to be freed eventually by mt76_mcu_skb_send_msg()…

item.title: CVE-2026-53101 — wifi: mt76: mt7921: fix potential deadlock in mt7921_roc_abort_sync
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: wifi: mt76: mt7921: fix potential deadlock in mt7921_roc_abort_sync roc_abort_sync() can deadlock with roc_work(). roc_work() holds dev->mt76.mutex, while cancel_work_sync() waits fo…

item.title: CVE-2026-53100 — wifi: mt76: fix deadlock in remain-on-channel
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: wifi: mt76: fix deadlock in remain-on-channel mt76_remain_on_channel() and mt76_roc_complete() call mt76_set_channel() while already holding dev->mutex. Since mt76_set_channel() also…

item.title: CVE-2026-53099 — bpf: Switch CONFIG_CFI_CLANG to CONFIG_CFI
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: bpf: Switch CONFIG_CFI_CLANG to CONFIG_CFI This was renamed in commit 23ef9d439769 ("kcfi: Rename CONFIG_CFI_CLANG to CONFIG_CFI") as it is now a compiler-agnostic option. Using the…

item.title: CVE-2026-53098 — wifi: mt76: mt7915: fix use-after-free bugs in mt7915_mac_dump_work()
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: wifi: mt76: mt7915: fix use-after-free bugs in mt7915_mac_dump_work() When the mt7915 pci chip is detaching, the mt7915_crash_data is released in mt7915_coredump_unregister(). Howeve…

item.title: CVE-2026-53097 — wifi: mt76: mt7996: fix use-after-free bugs in mt7996_mac_dump_work()
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: wifi: mt76: mt7996: fix use-after-free bugs in mt7996_mac_dump_work() When the mt7996 pci chip is detaching, the mt7996_crash_data is released in mt7996_coredump_unregister(). Howeve…

item.title: CVE-2026-53096 — bpf: Use RCU-safe iteration in dev_map_redirect_multi() SKB path
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: bpf: Use RCU-safe iteration in dev_map_redirect_multi() SKB path The DEVMAP_HASH branch in dev_map_redirect_multi() uses hlist_for_each_entry_safe() to iterate hash buckets, but this…

item.title: CVE-2026-53095 — bpf: Fix abuse of kprobe_write_ctx via freplace
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: bpf: Fix abuse of kprobe_write_ctx via freplace uprobe programs are allowed to modify struct pt_regs. Since the actual program type of uprobe is KPROBE, it can be abused to modify st…

item.title: CVE-2026-53094 — bpf: Fix stale offload->prog pointer after constant blinding
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: bpf: Fix stale offload->prog pointer after constant blinding When a dev-bound-only BPF program (BPF_F_XDP_DEV_BOUND_ONLY) undergoes JIT compilation with constant blinding enabled (bp…

item.title: CVE-2026-53093 — wifi: brcmfmac: Fix error pointer dereference
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: wifi: brcmfmac: Fix error pointer dereference The function brcmf_chip_add_core() can return an error pointer and is not checked. Add checks for error pointer. Detected by Smatch: dri…

item.title: CVE-2026-53092 — bpf: Fix linked reg delta tracking when src_reg == dst_reg
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: bpf: Fix linked reg delta tracking when src_reg == dst_reg Consider the case of rX += rX where src_reg and dst_reg are pointers to the same bpf_reg_state in adjust_reg_min_max_vals()…

item.title: CVE-2026-53091 — net: pull headers in qdisc_pkt_len_segs_init()
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: net: pull headers in qdisc_pkt_len_segs_init() Most ndo_start_xmit() methods expects headers of gso packets to be already in skb->head. net/core/tso.c users are particularly at risk,…

item.title: CVE-2026-53090 — bpf: Fix ld_{abs,ind} failure path analysis in subprogs
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: bpf: Fix ld_{abs,ind} failure path analysis in subprogs Usage of ld_{abs,ind} instructions got extended into subprogs some time ago via commit 09b28d76eac4 ("bpf: Add abnormal return…

item.title: CVE-2026-53089 — bpf: Fix use-after-free in offloaded map/prog info fill
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: bpf: Fix use-after-free in offloaded map/prog info fill When querying info for an offloaded BPF map or program, bpf_map_offload_info_fill_ns() and bpf_prog_offload_info_fill_ns() obt…

item.title: CVE-2026-53088 — net: bcmgenet: fix off-by-one in bcmgenet_put_txcb
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: net: bcmgenet: fix off-by-one in bcmgenet_put_txcb The write_ptr points to the next open tx_cb. We want to return the tx_cb that gets rewinded, so we must rewind the pointer first th…

item.title: CVE-2026-53087 — net: bcmgenet: fix leaking free_bds
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: net: bcmgenet: fix leaking free_bds While reclaiming the tx queue we fast forward the write pointer to drop any data in flight. These dropped frames are not added back to the pool of…

item.title: CVE-2026-53086 — net: bcmgenet: fix racing timeout handler
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: net: bcmgenet: fix racing timeout handler The bcmgenet_timeout handler tries to take down all tx queues when a single queue times out. This is over zealous and causes many race condi…

item.title: CVE-2026-53085 — bpf: fix mm lifecycle in open-coded task_vma iterator
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: bpf: fix mm lifecycle in open-coded task_vma iterator The open-coded task_vma iterator reads task->mm locklessly and acquires mmap_read_trylock() but never calls mmget(). If the task…

item.title: CVE-2026-53084 — bpf: return VMA snapshot from task_vma iterator
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: bpf: return VMA snapshot from task_vma iterator Holding the per-VMA lock across the BPF program body creates a lock ordering problem when helpers acquire locks that depend on mmap_lo…

item.title: CVE-2026-53083 — bpf: Fix RCU stall in bpf_fd_array_map_clear()
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: bpf: Fix RCU stall in bpf_fd_array_map_clear() Add a missing cond_resched() in bpf_fd_array_map_clear() loop. For PROG_ARRAY maps with many entries this loop calls prog_array_map_pok…

item.title: CVE-2026-53082 — net: hamradio: 6pack: fix uninit-value in sixpack_receive_buf
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: net: hamradio: 6pack: fix uninit-value in sixpack_receive_buf sixpack_receive_buf() does not properly skip bytes with TTY error flags. The while loop iterates through the flags buffe…

item.title: CVE-2026-53081 — bpf: Enforce regsafe base id consistency for BPF_ADD_CONST scalars
item.description: In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved: bpf: Enforce regsafe base id consistency for BPF_ADD_CONST scalars When regsafe() compares two scalar registers that both carry BPF_ADD_CONST, check_scalar_ids() maps their full comp…
